LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT GIAMPAOLO BOSCHETTI AKA PAUL BOSCHETTIS MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED. UDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: PAULA J. SHI, CSR #13358; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. AS TO THE DEMURRER ON UNCERTAINTY GROUNDS, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. AS TO THE DEMURRER ON THE GROUNDS OF FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP SEC. 377.32, THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE AND OVERRULES THE DEMURRER AS MOOT. AS TO THE DEMURRER TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. "THERE IS NO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES." (HILLARD V. A.H. ROBINS CO. (1983) 148 CAL.APP.3D 374, 391. RATHER, PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE A REMEDY. PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE THESE FACTS UNDER ONE OF THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: PAULA J. SHI, CSR #13358; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 57315824) FILED BY DEFENDANT BOSCHETTI, GIAMPAOLO AKA PAUL BOSCHETTI JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 7.0 DAYS
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-05-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER RE: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GIAMPAOLO BOSCHETTI, AKA PAUL BOSCHETTI'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION AS TO THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND THE ALLEGATIONS THAT SUPPORT THAT CLAIM. UNDER CIVIL CODE SECTION 3294, MALICE IS DEFINED AS EITHER (1) CONDUCT WHICH IS INTENDED BY THE DEFENDANT TO CAUSE INJURY TO THE PLAINTIFF OR (2) "DESPICABLE CONDUCT WHICH IS CARRIED ON BY THE DEFENDANT WITH A WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE RIGHTS OR SAFETY OF OTHERS." " 'DESPICABLE CONDUCT' IS CONDUCT WHICH IS SO VILE, BASE, CONTEMPTIBLE, MISERABLE, WRETCHED OR LOATHSOME THAT IT WOULD BE LOOKED DOWN UPON AND DESPISED BY ORDINARY DECENT PEOPLE." MOCK V. MICHIGAN MILLERS MUTUAL INS. CO. (1992) 4 CAL.APP.4TH 306, 331. HERE, PARAGRAPHS 11 AND 12 OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT PLEAD SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUSTAIN THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT WAS AWARE OF THE STOP SIGN, OBSERVED THE PEDESTRIANS, INTENTIONALLY FAILED TO STOP, AND CONTINUED TO PROCEED EVEN AFTER HE STRUCK DECEDENT. THE TRIER OF FACT COULD FIND THAT THE ALLEGED CONDUCT WAS DESPICABLE. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO STRIKE WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 6:28-7:3 AND 7:15-18 OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENDANT LIED TO THE POLICE AFTER THE ACCIDENT ARE IRRELEVANT AND DO NOT SUPPORT THE CLAIM. DEFENDANT CORRECTLY CITES FORD MOTOR CO. V. SUP. CRT. (1981) 120 CAL.APP.3D 748, 751, AND THE PARTIES APPEAR TO AGREE THAT PLAINTIFFS CANNOT RECOVER PUNITIVE DAMAGES ON THE WRONGFUL DEATH CLAIM. THERE IS NOTHING TO STRIKE, HOWEVER, BECAUSE IT APPEARS THAT THE PUNITIVES ARE BASED SOLELY ON THE SURVIVAL CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR # 7489 =302/EHG

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-11-2015 Continued To Jul-29-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MONICA BURNEIKIS OF THE LAW OFFICES OF MEISEL & KRENTSA, TEL. NO. 788-2035 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GIAMPAOLO BOSCHETTI, aka Paul Boschetti's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint - The motion to strike is granted and denied in part. The court denies the motion as to the claim for punitive damages and the allegations that support that claim. Under Civil Code section 3294, malice is defined as either (1) conduct which is intended by the defendant to cause injury to the plaintiff or (2) "despicable conduct which is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others." " 'Despicable conduct' is conduct which is so vile, base, contemptible, miserable, wretched or loathsome that it would be looked down upon and despised by ordinary decent people." Mock v. Michigan Millers Mutual Ins. Co. (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 306, 331. Here, paragraphs 11 and 12 of the 2nd amended complaint plead sufficient facts to sustain the claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff alleges that defendant was aware of the stop sign, observed the pedestrians, intentionally failed to stop, and continued to proceed even after he struck decedent. The trier of fact could find that the alleged conduct was despicable. The court grants the motion to strike without leave to amend as to 6:28-7:3 and 7:15-18 of the Second Amended Complaint. The allegations that defendant lied to the police after the accident are irrelevant and do not support the claim. Defendant correctly cites Ford Motor Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1981) 120 Cal.App.3d 748, 751, and the parties appear to agree that plaintiffs cannot recover punitive damages on the wrongful death claim. There is nothing to strike, however, because it appears that the punitives are based solely on the survival claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GIAMPAOLO BOSCHETTI AKA PAUL BOSCHETTIS'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is denied as moot in light of the ruling on demurrer. ORDER SIGNED. udge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Paula J. Shi, CSR #13358 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GIAMPAOLO BOSCHETTI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the demurrer on uncertainty grounds, the demurrer is overruled. The First Amended Complaint adequately pleads that the children of the decedent bring the first two causes of action under a wrongful death theory. The successor-in-interest of the estate brings the third cause of action under a survival theory that incorporates the allegations in the first two causes of action. As to the demurrer on the grounds of failure to comply with CCP sec. 377.32, the Court grants Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice and overrules the demurrer as moot. As to the demurrer to the punitive damages cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. "There is no cause of action for punitive damages." (Hillard v. A.H. Robins Co. (1983) 148 Cal.App.3d 374, 391. Rather, punitive damages are a remedy. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to allege these facts under one of the other causes of action. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Paula J. Shi, CSR #13358 (302/EHG)
